input.cmd: /bin/zsh -lc 'for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do curl -fsS --max-time 2 http://localhost:8080/orders >/dev/null && echo "probe $i ok" || echo "probe $i failed"; sleep 1; done'
/bin/zsh -lc "sleep 20; docker inspect sreft-orders --format '{{json .State}}'; docker logs --tail 20 sreft-orders"
/bin/zsh -lc 'sleep 20; for i in 1 2 3; do curl -fsS --max-time 2 http://localhost:8080/orders >/dev/null && echo "post-fix probe $i ok" || echo "post-fix probe $i failed"; done'

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /Users/peteshima/workplace/sre-field-tests/runs/subscription-20260710T221828Z/oom-killed__default__seed2__20260710T223420Z/submission.json <<'EOF'
{\"root_cause\":\"The orders container was configured with CHUNK_BYTES=1048576 and no CACHE_MAX, so every /orders request retained another 1 MiB chunk in an unbounded in-process cache. Under the sreft-load traffic this drove the container to its 256 MiB cgroup memory limit with swap disabled, causing Docker OOM kills and restart loops with exit code 137.\",\"actions_taken\":\"Inspected container state, logs, Docker OOMKilled status, memory limits, and compose configuration. Confirmed payments used the same image and memory cap but was stable with CACHE_MAX=16. Updated only the orders service environment in docker-compose.yaml to set CACHE_MAX=16, then recreated only sreft-orders with docker compose up -d --no-deps orders. Verified the service reported a bounded cache, stayed running without OOMKilled, served repeated /orders probes successfully, and held memory around 41 MiB of the 256 MiB limit under active load.\",\"postmortem\":\"A missing cache bound in orders allowed normal checkout traffic to translate directly into unbounded memory growth. The existing restart policy reduced downtime per crash but produced intermittent checkout failures because it did not address the leak. A bounded-cache setting already used by payments was the least-risk remediation and restored stability. Follow-ups should add configuration parity checks for services using this image, alert on OOMKilled/restart-loop conditions, and include a load test that verifies memory remains bounded before promotion.\"}
EOF
jq -e 'keys == [\"actions_taken\", \"postmortem\", \"root_cause\"] and (to_entries | all(.value | type == \"string\"))' /Users/peteshima/workplace/sre-field-tests/runs/subscription-20260710T221828Z/oom-killed__default__seed2__20260710T223420Z/submission.json && cat /Users/peteshima/workplace/sre-field-tests/runs/subscription-20260710T221828Z/oom-killed__default__seed2__20260710T223420Z/submission.json"
